sourceMovie.title: Logan
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Logan for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. James Mangold keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Amazing Spider-Man 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Logan clicked for you, The Amazing Spider-Man 2 (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Alita: Battle Angel
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Logan clicked for you, Alita: Battle Angel (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Logan clicked for you, Resident Evil (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Logan?
faq.answer: Try The Amazing Spider-Man 2, Alita: Battle Angel, Resident Evil—each shares DNA with Logan while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Logan?
faq.answer: Logan (2017) is best described as Action, Drama, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Logan part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Wolverine Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
